what_it_does: Records the results of an adversarial source-check pass over proposed design-system components (molecules and organisms) for the 'components' face, flipping a default-refuted verdict to 'stands' or 'corrected' only when the cited source slide was opened and the visual device was seen in the pixels.
observations: Applies a strict adversarial rule: default verdict is REFUTED; it is flipped to STANDS only on opened-slide pixel evidence.
Explicitly excludes live-DNA existence (slot_bindings, organisms.json keys, tokens.chrome) from counting as verification.

Most verdicts confirm existing live organisms, but several introduce new molecules/organisms such as pill, tag, glass_panel, status_dot, badge_pair, stat_orb, flow_pipeline, scope_box, zone_divider, and product_header.
Corrected entries narrow provenance or recipe rather than rejecting the device (scope_box, pill_row, status_dot, product_header).
Distinguishes visually similar devices by precise pixel traits, e.g., pill (fully rounded, interactive) versus tag (moderate radius, no fill, non-interactive).
Records a confirming_bias flag; only scope_box is flagged true.
Cites exact slide/page references across eight collections and includes parenthetical caveats where some cited slides were not opened.
standouts: Zero refutations under an adversarial default, with the headline calling the proposal unusually well-grounded.
The pill/tag split was vindicated by direct pixel inspection rather than assumed synonymy.
All four corrections are breadth or provenance corrections, not rejections of the underlying visual device.
Live-DNA existence is treated as not verification, forcing every confirmation back to opened-slide pixels.
conventions: Follows a structured verdict schema: name, status_claimed, verdict, source_evidence, confirming_bias.
Uses neutral, file-local language and avoids ranking or canonical claims.
Cites exact source slide/page identifiers for every verdict.
Breaks from trusting registry keys by requiring opened-slide visual evidence.
Distinguishes molecules, organisms, and faces and defers geometry/token details to other substrate files.
concerns: scope_box is flagged with confirming_bias=true, indicating its breadth claim was inflated.
Some verdicts rely on parenthetical caveats that a cited slide or variant was not opened, yet still stand.
Counts (n_stands, n_refuted, n_corrected) are manually tallied and could become inconsistent if the verdict list changes.
The 'law' excluding live-DNA verification is stated but not defined within this file.
notes: A pixel-level audit log that flips an adversarial default to confirm or correct design component proposals, tying every verdict to opened slide references.
questions: What is the precise schema distinction between a molecule, an organism, and a face in this ledger?
How are slide identifiers normalized across collections (e.g., p-02 versus p02)?
What downstream action is triggered by a 'corrected' verdict versus a 'stands' verdict?
Who is Tim and what is the ordinal-pill question raised for phase_strip?
Where is the 'law' that excludes live-DNA existence as verification documented?
purpose_vs_actual: matches — the file performs the adversarial source-check it describes and records verdicts, evidence, and counts accordingly.
apparent_themes: Adversarial verification with correction over rejection
Separation of registry existence from source-grounded verification
intention_signals: To produce an auditable, source-grounded ledger that shows which proposed UI components are visually confirmed in opened slides and which need their recipe or provenance corrected before acceptance.
novelty: An adversarial design-substrate verification report that defaults to refuted, requires opened-slide pixel evidence, and uses a confirming_bias flag plus non-binary corrected verdicts.
contribution: Provides a verified mapping of component names to opened-slide evidence and a set of corrected recipes/provenances that a fused system would retain to avoid overbroad or misattributed component claims.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON verification report for the 'components' face of a design system, recording adversarial source-check verdicts for 26 proposed UI molecules and organisms against opened slide pixels, with source evidence, bias flags, and counts.
intention_for_embedding: To audit and ground design component proposals in visible source-slide evidence before they are promoted into the canonical design substrate ledger.
